BG-1.name: INVOICE NOTE
BG-1.use: A group of business terms providing textual notes that are relevant for the invoice, together with an indication of the note subject.

BG-10.name: PAYEE
BG-10.usage_note: The role of Payee may be fulfilled by another party then the Seller, e.g. a factoring service.
BG-10.use: A group of business terms providing information about the Payee, i.e. the role that receives the payment.

BG-11.name: SELLER TAX REPRESENTATIVE PARTY
BG-11.use: A group of business terms providing information about the Seller's tax representative.

BG-12.name: SELLER TAX REPRESENTATIVE POSTAL ADDRESS
BG-12.usage_note: The seller tax representative name/postal address shall be provided in the invoice, if the seller has a tax representative who is liable to pay the VAT due. Sufficient components of the address are to be filled in order to comply to legal requirements.
BG-12.use: A group of business terms providing information about the postal address for the tax representative party.

BG-13.name: DELIVERY INFORMATION
BG-13.use: A group of business terms providing information about where and when the goods and services invoiced are delivered.

BG-14.name: INVOICING PERIOD
BG-14.usage_note: Used to indicate when the period covered by the invoice starts and when it ends. Also called delivery period.
BG-14.use: A group of business terms providing information on the invoice period.

BG-15.name: DELIVER TO ADDRESS
BG-15.usage_note: In the case of pick-up, the deliver to address is the pick-up address. Sufficient components of the address are to be filled to comply with legal requirements.
BG-15.use: A group of business terms providing information about the address to which goods and services invoiced were or are delivered.

BG-16.name: PAYMENT INSTRUCTIONS
BG-16.use: A group of business terms providing information about the payment.

BG-17.name: CREDIT TRANSFER
BG-17.use: A group of business terms to specify credit transfer payments.

BG-18.name: PAYMENT CARD INFORMATION
BG-18.usage_note: Only used if the Buyer has opted to pay by using a payment card such as a credit or debit card.
BG-18.use: A group of business terms providing information about card used for payment contemporaneous with invoice issuance.

BG-19.name: DIRECT DEBIT
BG-19.usage_note: This group may be used to give prior notice in the invoice that payment will be made through a SEPA or other direct debit initiated by the Seller, in accordance with the rules of the SEPA or other direct debit scheme.
BG-19.use: A group of business terms to specify a direct debit.

BG-2.name: PROCESS CONTROL
BG-2.use: A group of business terms providing information on the business process and rules applicable to the Invoice document.

BG-20.name: DOCUMENT LEVEL ALLOWANCES
BG-20.usage_note: Deductions, such as withheld tax may also be specified in this group.
BG-20.use: A group of business terms providing information about allowances applicable to the Invoice as a whole.

BG-21.name: DOCUMENT LEVEL CHARGES
BG-21.use: A group of business terms providing information about charges and taxes other than VAT, applicable to the Invoice as a whole.

BG-22.name: DOCUMENT TOTALS
BG-22.use: A group of business terms providing the monetary totals for the Invoice.

business_rules: BR-O-11 Sale is not subject to VAT/IGIC/IPSI
BG-23.name: VAT BREAKDOWN
BG-23.use: A group of business terms providing information about VAT breakdown by different categories, rates and exemption reasons

BG-24.name: ADDITIONAL SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS
BG-24.usage_note: The additional supporting documents can be used for both referencing a document number which is expected to be known by the receiver, an external document (referenced by a URL) or as an embedded document (such as a time report in pdf). The option to link to an external document will be needed, for example in the case of large attachments and/or when sensitive information, e.g. person-related services, has to be separated from the Invoice itself.
BG-24.use: A group of business terms providing information about additional supporting documents substantiating the claims made in the Invoice.

BG-25.name: INVOICE LINE
BG-25.use: A group of business terms providing information on individual Invoice lines.

BG-25-00.name: Grouping of information about the business transaction

BG-26.name: INVOICE LINE PERIOD
BG-26.usage_note: Is also called Invoice line delivery period.
BG-26.use: A group of business terms providing information about the Invoice period relevant for the Invoice line.

BG-27.name: INVOICE LINE ALLOWANCES
BG-27.use: A group of business terms providing information about allowances applicable to the individual Invoice line.

BG-28.name: INVOICE LINE CHARGES
BG-28.usage_note: All charges and taxes are assumed to be liable to the same VAT rate as the Invoice line.
BG-28.use: A group of business terms providing information about charges and taxes other than VAT applicable to the individual Invoice line.

BG-29.name: PRICE DETAILS
BG-29.synonyme: Aggregation of the contract information at line level
BG-29.use: A group of business terms providing information about the price applied for the goods and services invoiced on the Invoice line.

BG-3.name: PRECEDING INVOICE REFERENCE
BG-3.usage_note: To be used in case: - a preceding invoice is corrected - preceding partial invoices are refered to from a final invoice - preceding pre-payment invoices are refered to from a final invoice
BG-3.use: A group of business terms providing information on one or more preceding Invoices.

BG-30.name: LINE VAT INFORMATION
BG-30.use: A group of business terms providing information about the VAT applicable for the goods and services invoiced on the Invoice line.

BG-30-00.name: Grouping of billing information at line level
BG-30-00.use: Grouping of billing information at line level

BG-31.name: ITEM INFORMATION
BG-31.use: A group of business terms providing information about the goods and services invoiced.

BG-32.name: ITEM ATTRIBUTES
BG-32.use: A group of business terms providing information about properties of the goods and services invoiced.

BG-4.name: SELLER
BG-4.use: A group of business terms providing information about the Seller.

BG-5.name: SELLER POSTAL ADDRESS
BG-5.usage_note: Sufficient components of the address are to be filled in order to comply to legal requirements.
BG-5.use: A group of business terms providing information about the address of the Seller.

BG-6.name: SELLER CONTACT
BG-6.use: A group of business terms providing contact information about the Seller.

BG-7.name: BUYER
BG-7.use: A group of business terms providing information about the Buyer.

BG-8.name: BUYER POSTAL ADDRESS
BG-8.usage_note: Sufficient components of the address are to be filled in order to comply to legal requirements.
BG-8.use: A group of business terms providing information about the postal address for the Buyer.

BG-9.name: BUYER CONTACT
BG-9.usage_note: Contacting details can be given by the Buyer at the time of the ordering or as master data exchanged prior to ordering. It is recommended not to use contacting details for the purpose of routing the received Invoice internally by the recipient; the Buyer reference identifier should be used for this purpose.
BG-9.use: A group of business terms providing contact information relevant for the Buyer.

BG-X-12.name: Detailed information about the address of the final goods recipient

BG-X-15.use: Legal address of the buyerr in case the Buyer address is different

BG-X-21.name: Detailed information about the address of the deviating end user

BG-X-29.name: Detailed information about the address of the final recipient

BG-X-49.use: A group of business terms providing information about the Sales Agent

BG-X-73.name: Detailed information about the deviating invoice payer
BG-X-73.usage_note: The role of Payer may be fulfilled by another party than the Buyer, e.g. a third Party like a mother company

BG-X-76.name: Detailed information about the business address

BG-X-77.name: Deviating payee per payment
BG-X-77.usage_note: The role of beneficiary may be filled by a party other than the seller, e.g. a factoring service. THIS GROUP IS ONLY USED WHEN THERE ARE MULTIPLE BENEFICIARIES (e.g. withholding tax or split payment).

BG-X-85.name: PRECEDING INVOICE REFERENCE FOR ADVANCE PAYMENT
BG-X-85.usage_note: To be used in case: - preceding partial invoices are refered to from a final invoice - preceding pre-payment invoices are refered to from a final invoice
BG-X-85.use: A group of business terms providing information on the advance payment related preceding invoice. The individual invoide shall be stated so that combined payments need to be split per invoice.

BG-X-88.name: Delivery terms location
BG-X-88.use: A group of information elements containing information about the relevant location of the delivery terms.

BT-1.name: Invoice number
BT-1.usage_note: The sequential number required in Article 226(2) of the directive 2006/112/EC [2], to uniquely identify the Invoice within the business context, time-frame, operating systems and records of the Seller . It may be based on one or more series of numbers, which may include alphanumeric characters. No identification scheme is to be used.
BT-1.use: A unique identification of the Invoice.

BT-1-00.name: Grouping of characteristics that affect the entire document

BT-10.name: Buyer reference
BT-10.usage_note: The identifier is defined by the Buyer (e.g. contact ID, department, office id, project code), but provided by the Seller in the Invoice.
BT-10.use: An identifier assigned by the Buyer used for internal routing purposes.

BT-100.name: Document level charge base amount
BT-100.use: The base amount that may be used, in conjunction with the document level charge percentage, to calculate the document level charge amount.

BT-101.name: Document level charge percentage
BT-101.use: The percentage that may be used, in conjunction with the document level charge base amount, to calculate the document level charge amount.

BT-102.name: Document level charge VAT category code
BT-102.usage_note: The following entries of UNTDID 5305 [6] are used (further clarification between brackets): - Standard rate (Liable for VAT in a standard way) - Zero rated goods (Liable for VAT with a percentage rate of zero) - Exempt from tax (VAT/IGIC/IPSI) - VAT Reverse Charge (Reverse charge VAT/IGIC/IPSI rules apply) - VAT exempt for intra community supply of goods (VAT/IGIC/IPSI not levied due to Intra-community supply rules) - Free export item, tax not charged (VAT/IGIC/IPSI not levied due to export outside of the EU) - Services outside scope of tax (Sale is not subject to VAT/IGIC/IPSI) - Canary Islands General Indirect Tax (Liable for IGIC tax) - Liable for IPSI (Ceuta/Melilla tax)
BT-102.use: A coded identification of what VAT category applies to the document level charge.

BT-103.name: Document level charge VAT rate
BT-103.use: The VAT rate, represented as percentage that applies to the document level charge.

BT-104.name: Document level charge reason
BT-104.use: The reason for the document level charge, expressed as text.

BT-105.name: Document level charge reason code
BT-105.usage_note: Use entries of the UNTDID 7161 code list [6]. The Document level charge reason code and the Document level charge reason shall indicate the same charge reason.
BT-105.use: The reason for the document level charge, expressed as a code.

BT-106.use: Sum of all Invoice line net amounts in the Invoice.

BT-107.name: Sum of allowances on document level
BT-107.usage_note: Allowances on line level are included in the Invoice line net amount which is summed up into the Sum of Invoice line net amount.
BT-107.use: Sum of all allowances on document level in the Invoice.

business_rules: BR-FXEXT- CIUS Factur-X / ZUGFeRD Extended CO-12
BR-FXEXT- CIUS Factur-X / ZUGFeRD Extended CO-13
BT-108.name: Sum of charges on document level
BT-108.usage_note: Charges on line level are included in the Invoice line net amount which is summed up into the Sum of Invoice line net amount.
BT-108.use: Sum of all charges on document level in the Invoice.

business_rules: BR-FXEXT- CIUS Factur-X / ZUGFeRD Extended CO-13
BT-109.name: Invoice total amount without VAT
BT-109.usage_note: The Invoice total amount without VAT is the Sum of Invoice line net amount minus Sum of allowances on document level plus Sum of charges on document level.
BT-109.use: The total amount of the Invoice without VAT.

BT-11.name: Project reference
BT-11.use: The identification of the project the invoice refers to.

BT-11-0.name: Project name
BT-11-0.use: The identification of the project the invoice refers to

BT-110.name: Invoice total VAT amount
BT-110.usage_note: The Invoice total VAT amount is the sum of all VAT category tax amounts.
BT-110.use: The total VAT amount for the Invoice.

BT-111.name: Invoice total VAT amount in accounting currency
BT-111.usage_note: To be used when the VAT accounting currency (BT-6) differs from the Invoice currency code (BT-5) in accordance with article 230 of Directive 2006/112 / EC on VAT. The VAT amount in accounting currency is not used in the calculation of the Invoice totals.
BT-111.use: The VAT total amount expressed in the accounting currency accepted or required in the country of the Seller.

BT-112.name: Invoice total amount with VAT
BT-112.usage_note: The Invoice total amount with VAT is the Invoice total amount without VAT plus the Invoice total VAT amount.
BT-112.use: The total amount of the Invoice with VAT.

BT-113.name: Paid amount
BT-113.usage_note: This amount is subtracted from the invoice total amount with VAT to calculate the amount due for payment.
BT-113.use: The sum of amounts which have been paid in advance.

BT-114.name: Rounding amount
BT-114.use: The amount to be added to the invoice total to round the amount to be paid.

BT-115.name: Amount due for payment
BT-115.usage_note: This amount is the Invoice total amount with VAT minus the paid amount that has been paid in advance. The amount is zero in case of a fully paid Invoice. The amount may be negative; in that case the Seller owes the amount to the Buyer.
BT-115.use: The outstanding amount that is requested to be paid.

BT-122-0.name: Referenced document type

BT-123.name: Supporting document description
BT-123.usage_note: Such as: timesheet, usage report etc.
BT-123.use: A description of the supporting document.

BT-124.name: External document location
BT-124.usage_note: A means of locating the resource including its primary access mechanism, e.g. http:// or ftp://. External document location shall be used if the Buyer requires additional information to support the Invoice. External documents do not form part of the invoice. Risks can be involved when accessing external documents.
BT-124.use: The URL (Uniform Resource Locator) that identifies where the external document is located.

BT-125.name: Attached document
BT-125.usage_note: Attached document is used when documentation shall be stored with the Invoice for future reference or audit purposes.
BT-125.use: An attached document embedded as binary object or sent together with the invoice.

BT-125-1.name: Attached document Mime code
BT-125-1.usage_note: Allowed mime codes: - application/pdf - image/png - image/jpeg - text/csv - application/vnd.openxmlformatsofficedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet - application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.spreadsheet
BT-125-1.use: The mime code of the attached document. application/json json application/pdf pdf application/vnd.oasis. vnd.oasis.opendocument.spreadsheet application/xml xml image/jpeg image/png

BT-125-2.name: Attached document Filename
BT-125-2.use: The file name of the attached document

BT-126.name: Invoice line identifier
BT-126.use: A unique identifier for the individual line within the Invoice.

BT-127.name: Invoice line note

BT-127-00.name: Detailed information about the free text of the line item

BT-128.name: Invoice line object identifier
BT-128.usage_note: It may be a subscription number, telephone number, meter point etc., as applicable.
BT-128.use: An identifier for an object on which the invoice line is based, given by the Seller.

BT-128-00.name: Object identifier at the invoice item level

BT-128-1.name: Scheme identifier Invocice line object identifier
BT-128-1.usage_note: If it may be not clear for the receiver what scheme is used for the identifier, a conditional scheme identifier should be used that shall be chosen from the UNTDID 1153 code list [6] entries. TypeCode = "130" invoice data sheet
BT-128-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Invoice line object identifier.

BT-129.name: Invoiced quantity
BT-129.use: The quantity of items (goods or services) that is charged in the Invoice line.

BT-129-00.name: Grouping of delivery details on line level
BT-129-00.use: Grouping of delivery details on line level

BT-13.name: Purchase order reference
BT-13.use: An identifier of a referenced purchase order, issued by the Buyer.

BT-130.name: Invoiced quantity unit of measure
BT-130.usage_note: The unit of measure shall be chosen from the lists in UN/ECE Recommendation N°. 20 “Codes for Units of Measure Used in International Trade” [7] and UN/ECE Recommendation N° 21 “Codes for Passengers, Types of Cargo, Packages and Packaging Materials (with Complementary Codes for Package Names)” [19] applying the method described in UN/ECE Rec N° 20 Intro 2.a). Note that in most cases it is not needed for Buyers and Sellers to implement these lists fully in their software. Sellers need only to support the units needed for their goods and services; Buyers only need to verify that the units used in the Invoice are equal to the units used in other documents (such as Contract, Catalogue, Order and Despatch advice).
BT-130.use: The unit of measure that applies to the invoiced quantity.

BT-131.name: Invoice line net amount
BT-131.usage_note: The amount is “net” without VAT, i.e. inclusive of line level allowances and charges as well as other relevant taxes.
BT-131.use: The total amount of the Invoice line.

BT-132.name: Referenced purchase order line reference
BT-132.usage_note: The purchase order identifier is referenced on document level.
BT-132.use: An identifier for a referenced line within a purchase order, issued by the Buyer.

BT-133.name: Invoice line Buyer accounting reference
BT-133.usage_note: If required, this reference shall be provided by the Buyer to the Seller prior to the issuing of the Invoice.
BT-133.use: A textual value that specifies where to book the relevant data into the Buyer's financial accounts.

BT-133-00.name: Detailed information on the accounting reference

BT-134.name: Invoice line period start date
BT-134.usage_note: The date is the first day of the period.
BT-134.use: The date when the Invoice period for this Invoice line starts.

BT-134-00.name: Start of the invoice line billing period

BT-135.name: Invoice line period end date
BT-135.usage_note: The date is the last day of the period.
BT-135.use: The date when the Invoice period for this Invoice line ends.

BT-135-00.name: End of the invoice line billing period

BT-136.name: Invoice line allowance amount
BT-136.use: The amount of an allowance, without VAT.

BT-137.name: Invoice line allowance base amount
BT-137.use: The base amount that may be used, in conjunction with the Invoice line allowance percentage, to calculate the Invoice line allowance amount.

BT-138.name: Invoice line allowance percentage
BT-138.use: The percentage that may be used, in conjunction with the Invoice line allowance base amount, to calculate the Invoice line allowance amount.

BT-139.name: Invoice line allowance reason
BT-139.use: The reason for the Invoice line allowance, expressed as text.

BT-14.name: Sales order reference
BT-14.use: An identifier of a referenced sales order, issued by the Seller.

BT-14-00.name: Details about the associated order confirmation

BT-140.name: Invoice line allowance reason code
BT-140.usage_note: Use entries of the UNTDID 5189 code list [6]. The Invoice line level allowance reason code and the Invoice line level allowance reason shall indicate the same allowance reason.
BT-140.use: The reason for the Invoice line allowance, expressed as a code.

BT-141.name: Invoice line charge amount
BT-141.use: The amount of a charge, without VAT.

BT-142.name: Invoice line charge base amount
BT-142.use: The base amount that may be used, in conjunction with the Invoice line charge percentage, to calculate the Invoice line charge amount.

BT-143.name: Invoice line charge percentage
BT-143.use: The percentage that may be used, in conjunction with the Invoice line charge base amount, to calculate the Invoice line charge amount.

BT-144.name: Invoice line charge reason
BT-144.use: The reason for the Invoice line charge, expressed as text.

BT-145.name: Invoice line charge reason code
BT-145.usage_note: Use entries of the UNTDID 7161 code list [6]. The Invoice line charge reason code and the Invoice line charge reason shall indicate the same charge reason.
BT-145.use: The reason for the Invoice line charge, expressed as a code.

BT-146.name: Item net price
BT-146.usage_note: The Item net price has to be equal with the Item gross price less the Item price discount.
BT-146.use: The price of an item, exclusive of VAT, after subtracting item price discount.

BT-146-00.name: Detailed information on the net price of the item
BT-146-00.usage_note: The net price includes all surchages and discounts, except for VAT.

BT-147.name: Item price discount
BT-147.usage_note: Only applies if the discount is provided per unit and if it is not included in the Item gross price.
BT-147.use: The total discount subtracted from the Item gross price to calculate the Item net price.

BT-147-00.name: Price-related discounts
BT-147-00.use: Detailed information on discounts and charges

BT-148.name: Item gross price
BT-148.use: The unit price, exclusive of VAT, before subtracting Item price discount.

BT-148-00.name: Detailed information on the gross price of the item

BT-149.name: Item price base quantity
BT-149.use: The number of item units to which the price applies.

BT-149-1.name: Item price base quantity
BT-149-1.use: The number of item units to which the price applies.

BT-15.name: Receiving advice reference
BT-15.use: An identifier of a referenced receiving advice.

BT-15-00.name: Detailed information about the associated goods receipt

BT-150.name: Item price base quantity unit of measure code
BT-150.usage_note: The Item price base quantity unit of measure shall be the same as the Invoiced quantity unit of measure (BT- 130).
BT-150.use: The unit of measure that applies to the Item price base quantity.

BT-150-1.name: Item price base quantity unit of measure code
BT-150-1.usage_note: The Item price base quantity unit of measure shall be the same as the Invoiced quantity unit of measure (BT- 130).
BT-150-1.use: The unit of measure that applies to the Item price base quantity.

BT-151.name: Invoiced item VAT category code
BT-151.usage_note: The following entries of UNTDID 5305 [6] are used (further clarification between brackets): - Standard rate (Liable for VAT in a standard way) - Zero rated goods (Liable for VAT with a percentage rate of zero) - Exempt from tax (VAT/IGIC/IPSI) - VAT Reverse Charge (Reverse charge VAT/IGIC/IPSI rules apply) - VAT exempt for intra community supply of goods (VAT/IGIC/IPSI not levied due to Intra-community supply rules) - Free export item, tax not charged (VAT/IGIC/IPSI not levied due to export outside of the EU) - Services outside scope of tax (Sale is not subject to VAT/IGIC/IPSI) - Canary Islands General Indirect Tax (Liable for IGIC tax) - Liable for IPSI (Ceuta/Melilla tax)
BT-151.use: The VAT category code for the invoiced item.

BT-151-0.name: Invoiced item VAT category code, Content
BT-151-0.usage_note: For more information on the recommended codes, please refer to subclause 6.3.3.2 - Specification of VAT category codes.
BT-151-0.use: The VAT category code for the invoiced item.

BT-152.name: Invoiced item VAT rate
BT-152.use: The VAT rate, represented as percentage that applies to the invoiced item.

BT-154.name: Item description
BT-154.usage_note: The Item description allows for describing the item and its features in more detail than the Item name.

BT-155.name: Item Seller's identifier
BT-155.use: An identifier, assigned by the Seller, for the item.

BT-156.name: Item Buyer's identifier
BT-156.use: An identifier, assigned by the Buyer, for the item.

BT-157.name: Item standard identifier
BT-157.use: An item identifier based on a registered scheme.

BT-157-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-157-1.usage_note: The identification scheme shall be identified from the entries of the list published by the ISO/IEC 6523 maintenance agency.
BT-157-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Item standard identifier

BT-158.name: Item classification identifier
BT-158.usage_note: Classification codes are used to allow grouping of similar items for a various purposes e.g. public procurement (CPV), e-Commerce (UNSPSC) etc.
BT-158.use: A code for classifying the item by its type or nature.

BT-158-00.name: Detailed information on the item classification

BT-158-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-158-1.usage_note: The identification scheme shall be chosen from the entries in UNTDID 7143 [6].
BT-158-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Item classification identifier

BT-158-2.name: Scheme version identifer
BT-158-2.use: The version of the identification scheme.

BT-159.name: Item country of origin
BT-159.usage_note: The lists of valid countries are registered with the ISO 3166-1 Maintenance agency, "Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions".
BT-159.use: The code identifying the country from which the item originates.

BT-159-00.name: Detailed information on the item origin

BT-16.name: Despatch advice reference
BT-16.use: An identifier of a referenced despatch advice.

BT-16-00.name: Detailed information on the corresponding despatch advice

BT-160.name: Item attribute name
BT-160.use: The name of the attribute or property of the item.

BT-161.name: Item attribute value
BT-161.use: The value of the attribute or property of the item.

BT-162.name: Seller address line 3
BT-162.use: An additional address line in an address that can be used to give further details supplementing the main line.

BT-163.name: Buyer address line 3
BT-163.use: An additional address line in an address that can be used to give further details supplementing the main line.

BT-164.name: Tax representative address line 3
BT-164.use: An additional address line in an address that can be used to give further details supplementing the main line.

BT-165.name: Deliver to address line 3
BT-165.use: An additional address line in an address that can be used to give further details supplementing the main line.

BT-17.name: Tender or lot reference
BT-17.usage_note: In some countries a reference to the call for tender that has led to the contract shall be provided.
BT-17.use: The identification of the call for tender or lot the invoice relates to.

BT-17-0.name: Referenced document type
BT-17-0.usage_note: The code 50 "Price/sales catalogue response" shall be used to refer to the tendering or batch. (BT-17)

BT-17-00.name: Details on tender or lot reference

BT-18.name: Invoiced object identifier
BT-18.usage_note: It may be a subscription number, telephone number, meter point, vehicle, person etc., as applicable.
BT-18.use: An identifier for an object on which the invoice is based, given by the Seller.

BT-18-0.name: Referenced document type
BT-18-0.usage_note: The code 130 "invoice data" shall be used to refer to the identifier of an object given by the seller (BT-18).

BT-18-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-18-1.usage_note: If it may be not clear for the receiver what scheme is used for the identifier, a onditional scheme identifier should be used that shall be chosen from the UNTDID 1153 code list [6] entries.
BT-18-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Invoiced object identifier.

BT-19.name: Buyer accounting reference
BT-19.use: A textual value that specifies where to book the relevant data into the Buyer's financial accounts.

BT-19-00.name: Detailed information about the accounting reference

BT-2.name: Invoice issue date
BT-2.use: The date when the Invoice was issued.

BT-2-00.name: Invoice issue date, Content
BT-2-00.use: The date when the Invoice was issued.

BT-20.name: Payment terms
BT-20.usage_note: This element may contain multiple lines and multiple terms.
BT-20.use: A textual description of the payment terms that apply to the amount due for payment (Including description of possible penalties).

BT-21.name: Invoice note subject code
BT-21.usage_note: To be chosen from the entries in UNTDID 4451 [6].
BT-21.use: The subject of the textual note in BT-22.

BT-22.name: Invoice note
BT-22.usage_note: Such as the reason for any correction or assignment note in case the invoice has been factored.
BT-22.use: A textual note that gives unstructured information that is relevant to the Invoice as a whole.

BT-23.name: Business process type
BT-23.usage_note: To be specifid by the Buyer.
BT-23.use: Identifies the business process context in which the transaction appears, to enable the Buyer to process the Invoice in an appropriate way.

BT-24.name: Specification identifier
BT-24.usage_note: This identifies compiance or conformance to this document. Conformant invoices specify: urn:cen.eu:en16931: 2017. Invoices, compliant to a user specification may identify that user specification here. No identification scheme is to be used.
BT-24.use: An identification of the specification containing the total set of rules regarding semantic content, cardinalities and business rules to which the data contained in the instance document conforms. urn:cen.eu:en16931: Profile EXTENDED 1p0:extended urn:cen.eu:en16931: ZUGFeRD 2.0 deprecated Profile EXTENDED, should not be used 2p0:extended

BT-25.name: Preceding Invoice reference
BT-25.use: The identification of an Invoice that was previously sent by the Seller.

BT-26.name: Preceding Invoice issue date
BT-26.usage_note: The Preceding Invoice issue date shall be provided in case the Preceding Invoice identifier is not unique.
BT-26.use: The date when the Preceding Invoice was issued.

BT-27.name: Seller name
BT-27.use: The full formal name by which the Seller is registered in the national registry of legal entities or as a Taxable person or otherwise trades as a person or persons.

BT-28.name: Seller trading name
BT-28.usage_note: This may be used if different from the Seller name.
BT-28.use: A name by which the Seller is known, other than Seller name (also known as Business name).

BT-29.name: Seller identifier
BT-29.usage_note: For many systems, the Seller identifier is a key piece of information. Multiple Seller identifiers may be assigned or specified. They may be differentiated by using various identification schemes. If no scheme is specified, it should be known by Buyer and Seller, e.g. a previously exchanged Buyer assigned identifier of the Seller.

BT-29-0.name: Seller global identifier
BT-29-0.usage_note: The seller’s global ID is a unique identifier assigned to a seller by a global registration body.
BT-29-0.use: The identification scheme identifier of the seller is a specific identifier given to the seller by a global registration authority.

BT-29-1.name: Seller identifier identification scheme identifier
BT-29-1.usage_note: The identification scheme identifier shall be chosen from the entries of the list published by the ISO 6523 maintenance agency.

BT-3.name: Invoice type code
BT-3.usage_note: Commercial invoices and credit notes are defined according the entries in UNTDID 1001 [6]. Other entries of UNTDID 1001 [6] with specific invoices or credit notes may be used if applicable.
BT-3.use: A code specifying the functional type of the Invoice.

BT-30.name: Seller legal registration identifier
BT-30.usage_note: If no identification scheme is specified, it must be known by Buyer and Seller.
BT-30.use: An identifier issued by an official registrar that identifies the Seller as a legal entity or person.

BT-30-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-30-1.usage_note: If used, the identification scheme shall be chosen from the entries of the list published by the ISO/IEC 6523 maintenance agency.
BT-30-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Seller legal registration identifier.

BT-31.usage_note: VAT number prefixed by a country code. A VAT registered Supplier shall include his VAT ID, except when he uses a tax representative.
BT-31.use: The Seller's VAT identifier (also known as Seller VAT identification number).

BT-31-00.name: Detailed information on tax information of the seller

BT-32.name: Seller tax registration identifier
BT-32.usage_note: This information may affect how the Buyer settles the payment (such as for social security fees). E.g. in some countries, if the Seller is not registered as a tax paying entity then the Buyer is required to withhold the amount of the tax and pay it on behalf of the Seller.
BT-32.use: The local identification (defined by the Seller’s address) of the Seller for tax purposes or a reference that enables the Seller to state his registered tax status.

BT-32-00.name: Detailed information on tax information of the seller

BT-33.name: Seller additional legal information
BT-33.use: Additional legal information relevant for the Seller.

BT-34.name: Seller electronic address
BT-34.use: Identifies the Seller's electronic address to which a business document may be delivered.

BT-34-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-34-1.usage_note: The scheme identifier shall be chosen from a list to be maintained by the Connecting Europe Facility.
BT-34-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Seller electronic address

BT-35.name: Seller address line 1
BT-35.usage_note: Usually the street name and number or post office box.
BT-35.use: The main address line in an address.

BT-36.name: Seller address line 2
BT-36.use: An additional address line in an address that can be used to give further details supplementing the main line.

BT-37.name: Seller city
BT-37.use: The common name of the city, town or village, where the Seller address is located.

BT-38.name: Seller post code
BT-38.usage_note: Such as a ZIP code or a post code.
BT-38.use: The identifier for an addressable group of properties according to the relevant postal service.

BT-39.name: Seller country subdivision
BT-39.usage_note: Such as a region, a county, a state, a province, etc.

BT-40.name: Seller country code
BT-40.usage_note: If no tax representative is specified, this is the country where VAT is liable. The lists of valid countries are registered with the ISO 3166-1 Maintenance agency, "Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions".
BT-40.use: A code that identifies the country.

BT-41.name: Seller contact point
BT-41.usage_note: Such as person name, contact identification, department or office identification.
BT-41.use: A contact point for a legal entity or person.

BT-42.name: Seller contact telephone number
BT-42.use: A phone number for the contact point.

BT-42-00.name: Detailed information about the seller phone number

BT-43.name: Seller contact email address
BT-43.use: An e-mail address for the contact point.

BT-43-00.name: Detailed information about the seller email address

BT-44.name: Buyer name
BT-44.use: The full name of the Buyer.

BT-45.name: Buyer trading name
BT-45.usage_note: This may be used if different from the Buyer name.
BT-45.use: A name by which the Buyer is known, other than Buyer name (also known as Business name).

BT-46.name: Buyer identifier
BT-46.usage_note: If no scheme is specified, it must be known by Buyer and Seller.

BT-46-0.name: Buyer global identifier Buyer global identifier
BT-46-0.usage_note: GloablID, if global identifier exists and can be stated in @schemeID, ID else The global identifier of a buyer is the specific identification given to him by a global registry organization.
BT-46-0.use: The identification scheme identifier of the seller is a specific identifier given to the seller by a global registration authority.

BT-46-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-46-1.usage_note: If used, the identification scheme shall be chosen from the entries of the list published by the ISO/IEC 6523 maintenance agency.
BT-46-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Buyer identifier.

BT-47.name: Buyer legal registration identifier
BT-47.usage_note: If no identification scheme is specified, it should be known by Buyer and Seller, e.g. the identifier that is exclusively used in the applicable legal environment.
BT-47.use: An identifier issued by an official registrar that identifies the Buyer as a legal entity or person.

BT-47-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-47-1.usage_note: If used, the identification scheme shall be chosen from the entries of the list published by the ISO 6523 maintenance agency.
BT-47-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Buyer legal registration identifier.

BT-58.name: Buyer contact email address

The lists of valid currencies are registered with the ISO 4217 Maintenance Agency "Codes for the representation of currencies and funds". Please refer to Article 230 of the Council Directive 2006/112/EC [2] for more information.
BT-6.use: The currency used for VAT accounting and reporting purposes as accepted or required in the country of the Seller.

BT-60.name: Payee identifier

BT-60-1.name: Scheme identifier

BT-61.name: Payee legal registration identifier

BT-61.use: An identifier issued by an official registrar that identifies the Payee as a legal entity or person.

BT-61-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-61-1.usage_note: If used, the identification scheme shall be chosen from the entries of the list published by the ISO/IEC 6523 maintenance agency.
BT-61-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Payee legal registration identifier.

BT-62.name: Seller tax representative name
BT-62.use: The full name of the Seller's tax representative party.

BT-7.name: Value added tax point date
BT-7.usage_note: The tax point is usually the date goods were supplied or services completed (the 'basic tax point'). There are some variations. Please refer to Article 226 (7) of the Council Directive 2006/112/EC [2] for more information. This element is required if the Value added tax point date is different from the Invoice issue date. Both Buyer and Seller should use the Tax Point Date when provided by the Seller. The use of BT-7 and BT-8 is mutually exclusive.
BT-7.use: The date when the VAT becomes accountable for the Seller and for the Buyer in so far as that date can be determined and differs from the date of issue of the invoice, according to the VAT directive.

BT-7-00.name: Tax due date
BT-7-00.usage_note: This does not apply in Germany. Use date of delivery instead.

BT-71-1.name: Scheme identifier
BT-71-1.usage_note: If used, the identification scheme shall be chosen from the entries of the list published by the ISO/IEC 6523 maintenance agency.
BT-71-1.use: The identification scheme identifier of the Deliver to location identifier.

BT-8.usage_note: The code shall distinguish between the following entries of UNTDID 2005 [6]: - Invoice docment issue date - Delivery date, actual - Payment date. The Value added tax point date code is used if the Value added tax point date is not known when the invoice is issued. The use of BT-8 and BT-7 is mutually exclusive.

BT-81.usage_note: Entries from the UNTDID 4461 code list [6] shall be used. Distinction should be made between SEPA and non-SEPA payments, and between credit payments, direct debits, card payments and other instruments.
BT-81.use: The means, expressed as code, for how a payment is expected to be or has been settled.

BT-82.name: Payment means text
BT-82.usage_note: Such as cash, credit transfer, direct debit, credit card, etc.
BT-82.use: The means, expressed as text, for how a payment is expected to be or has been settled.

BT-83.name: Remittance information
BT-83.usage_note: Used for creditor's critical reconciliation information. This information element helps the Seller to assign an incoming payment to the relevant payment process. When specifying the textual value, which is commonly the invoice number of the invoice being paid, but may be another seller reference, the buyer should indicate this reference in his payment order when executing the payment. In a payment transaction this reference is transferred back to the Seller as Remittance Information. In order to allow for automatic processing of cross-border SEPA payments, only Latin characters should be used in this field, with a maximum of 140 characters. Referencesection 1.4 of the SEPA credit transfer and SEPA direct debit scheme implementation guides [13] and [14] for details of the allowed characters. Other rules may apply for SEPA payments within national borders. If remittance information is structured according to the ISO 11649:2009 standard [16] for Structured RF Creditor Reference, it shall be mapped to the Structured Remittance Information Creditor Reference field in SEPA payments messages. If remittance information is structured according to the EACT standard for automated reconciliation [17], it shall be mapped to the Unstructured Remittance Information field in SEPA payments messages.” If remittance information is to be mapped to the End To End Identification field or to the Structured Remittance Information Creditor Reference field in SEPA payments messages, then in addition to the Latin character set restriction, the content shall not start or end with a '/' and the content shall not contain '//'s. See reference [15].
BT-83.use: A textual value used to establish a link between the payment and the Invoice, issued by the Seller.

BT-84.name: Payment account identifier
BT-84.usage_note: Such as IBAN (in case of SEPA payment) or a national account number.
BT-84.use: A unique identifier of the financial payment account, at a payment service provider, to which payment should be made.

BT-84-0.name: National account number (not SEPA)
BT-84-0.usage_note: Use IBANID when appropriate, otherwise use ProprietaryID

BT-85.name: Payment account name
BT-85.use: The name of the payment account, at a payment service provider, to which payment should be made.

BT-86.name: Payment service provider identifier
BT-86.usage_note: Such as a BIC or a national clearing code where required. No identification scheme to be used.
BT-86.use: An identifier for the payment service provider where a payment account is located.

BT-87.name: Payment card primary account number
BT-87.usage_note: In accordance with card payments security standards an invoice should never include a full card primary account number. At the moment PCI Security Standards Council has defined following: The first 6 digits and last 4 digits are the maximum number of digits to be shown.
BT-87.use: The Primary Account Number (PAN) of the card used for payment.

BT-88.name: Payment card holder name
BT-88.use: The name of the payment card holder.

BT-89.name: Mandate reference identifier
BT-89.usage_note: Used in order to pre-notify the Buyer of a SEPA direct debit.
BT-89.use: Unique identifier assigned by the Payee for referencing the direct debit mandate.

BT-9.name: Payment due date
BT-9.usage_note: The payment due date reflects the due date of the net payment. For partial payments it states the first net due date. The corresponding description of more complex payment terms can be stated in BT-20 Payment terms.
BT-9.use: The date when the payment is due.

BT-90.use: Unique banking reference identifier of the Payee or Seller assigned by the Payee or Seller bank.

BT-91.use: The account to be debited by the direct debit.

BT-X-296.name: VAT category code
BT-X-296.usage_note: The following entries of UNTDID 5305 [6] are used (further clarification between brackets): - Standard rate (Liable for VAT in a standard way) - Zero rated goods (Liable for VAT with a percentage rate of zero) - Exempt from tax (VAT/IGIC/IPSI) - VAT Reverse Charge (Reverse charge VAT/IGIC/IPSI rules apply) - VAT exempt for intra community supply of goods (VAT/IGIC/IPSI not levied due to Intra-community supply rules) - Free export item, tax not charged (VAT/IGIC/IPSI not levied due to export outside of the EU) - Services outside scope of tax (Sale is not subject to VAT/IGIC/IPSI) - Canary Islands General Indirect Tax (Liable for IGIC tax) - Liable for IPSI (Ceuta/Melilla tax)
BT-X-296.use: Coded identification of a VAT category.

BT-X-297.name: VAT exemption reason code
BT-X-297.usage_note: Code list issued and maintained by the Connecting Europe Facility.

BT-X-298.name: VAT category rate
BT-X-298.usage_note: The VAT category code and the VAT category rate shall be consistent.
